canonicalTerms.definition: A Claude Code plugin that provides local-first news intelligence by collecting, deduplicating, clustering, scoring, and presenting news articles as a ranked digest.
The primary output of the Herald pipeline: a ranked, grouped summary of top news stories written as a structured YAML or Markdown file, one per day.
Herald v2's 4-stage processing sequence: Collect → Ingest → Cluster → Project. Orchestrated by herald.cli run (Python) and invoked by the scheduler or manually via /news-run.
A cluster of one or more related articles grouped by title similarity. The canonical unit in Herald v2's data model, stored in the stories table with a composite score.
A single fetched news item from an RSS feed, HN Algolia API, or Tavily search result, stored with a canonical URL, score, and source reference.
A configured news origin — an RSS/Atom feed URL, the HN Algolia API, or the Tavily web search adapter — with an associated tier weight and topic category.
A curated bundle of feed sources for a subject area (e.g., ai-engineering, rust, security). Built-in packs are bundled as immutable YAML presets in the Herald package.
An immutable YAML configuration file bundled with Herald that defines feed sources and keyword defaults for a topic pack. Never modified by the user; updated on plugin upgrade.
A numeric ranking (1–4) assigned to each source that determines its base weight in the scoring formula. Tier 1 (weight 3.0) = highest quality; Tier 4 (weight 0.5) = high-volume, less curated.
The v2 algorithm that ranks articles: source_weight + min(points/500, 3.0). Story-level score adds coverage (mention count) + momentum. Recency decay is applied separately.

Herald v2's URL-based deduplication in the Ingest stage: URL canonicalization (strip tracking params, normalize hosts, sort query params) + UPSERT on canonical URL. Title similarity (threshold 0.65) is handled separately by the Cluster stage.

The Cluster stage of the pipeline that groups related articles into stories using Python SequenceMatcher title similarity, with merge guards for threshold, time gap, title length, and version conflicts.
A Claude Code hook that fires at the beginning of every session, checks for today's digest, and prints a one-line reminder if it exists or a warning if the last fetch is more than 24 hours old.
A Claude Code skill that auto-activates when the conversation contains trigger phrases and inlines a formatted summary from the most recent digest.
Legacy v1 shell orchestrator (src/pipeline/run.sh). In v2, the pipeline is orchestrated by herald.cli run (Python), which calls collect → ingest → cluster → project stages directly.
Herald v2 collect module (herald/collect.py) that fetches raw content from RSS/Atom feeds (via httpx), HN Algolia API, and optionally Tavily. Returns RawItem objects for the Ingest stage.
Herald v2 ingest module (herald/ingest.py) that deduplicates articles by canonical URL, UPSERTs into SQLite, tracks cross-source mentions, and assigns topics by keyword matching.
Legacy v1 analysis module (src/pipeline/analyze.py). In v2, scoring is handled by herald/scoring.py and projection by herald/project.py.
The platform-native job runner that invokes run.sh on a schedule (default: daily at 07:00). Implemented as a launchd plist (macOS), systemd user timer (Linux), or crontab entry (fallback).
Legacy v1 concept (/tmp/herald.lock used by run.sh). In v2, pipeline concurrency is managed by SQLite WAL mode and atomic DB transactions — no external lockfile.
An optional web search API adapter used by collect.py for supplemental web results. Only activated when TAVILY_API_KEY is set. Not required for core functionality.
A v2 data model record tracking when an article URL is referenced by multiple sources, enabling cross-source mention counting as a scoring signal.
SQLite Full-Text Search extension used in Herald v2 to index articles and stories for fast keyword search. Synchronized with base tables via triggers.
In Herald v2, the Markdown output file (with YAML frontmatter) produced by the Project stage, stored as ~/.herald/briefs/{run_id}.md. Equivalent to 'digest' in v1 terminology.
The Heurema plugin marketplace from which Herald is installed via `claude plugin marketplace add heurema/emporium`.
A time-based scoring component that reduces an article's score as it ages: 1.0 at publish time, 0.5 at 12 hours, 0.1 at 24 hours.
